results.note: Atlas of 368 Dot/Icm (T4SS) effectors; neither gene 'MavL' nor locus lpg2526 appears in retrieved full text (listed only in supplementary dataset that did not extract). No verbatim quote available.

results.resolved_title: Proteolytic elimination of N-myristoyl modifications by the Shigella virulence factor IpaJ
results.quote: an irreversible mechanism of protein demyristoylation catalysed by invasion plasmid antigen J (IpaJ), a previously uncharacterized Shigella flexneri type III effector protein with cysteine protease activity
results.note: Cited DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2012.10.011 did not resolve to a PMID/PMCID and could not be retrieved. Quote is from the Burnaevskiy et al. 2013 Nature paper on the same protein, PMID 23535599, DOI 10.1038/nature12004; confirms IpaJ is a Shigella T3SS effector.

results.resolved_title: Evidence for the secretion of Chlamydia trachomatis CopN by a type III secretion mechanism.
results.quote: The observation that CopN is also secreted by the Yersinia type III apparatus provides support for the notion that chlamydiae secrete proteins via a type III mechanism.
results.note: Cited paper (PMID 11123678); abstract directly states CopN is a secreted T3SS substrate of C. trachomatis. Full text behind paywall; abstract sufficient.

results.resolved_title: Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae haemolysin II is secreted from Escherichia coli by A. pleuropneumoniae pleurotoxin secretion gene products
results.quote: These genes facilitated the export of type II haemolysin from E. coli, and may perform this function in A. pleuropneumoniae.
results.note: Type II haemolysin = ApxII (apxIIA). Abstract shows it is secreted by the Apx (pleurotoxin) secretion gene products, the dedicated RTX/ABC-transporter (T1SS) machinery. Cited paper retrieved via OUP abstract; DOI 10.1111/j.1574-6968.1993.tb06187.x is the resolved form of the legacy DOI.

results.resolved_title: Mutagenesis and isolation of Aeromonas hydrophila genes which are required for extracellular secretion.
results.note: Cited paper (PMID 1846860) characterizes the A. hydrophila GSP/T2SS secretion machinery via aerolysin and protease; it does not name amylase/amyA (lpg n/a) as a substrate. UniProt P41131 only lists AmyA as 'Secreted' without naming a secretion system. No paper-level evidence that amyA is the T2SS substrate.

results.resolved_title: Observation of dynamic non-Hermitian skin effects
results.note: DOI MISMATCH: 10.1038/s41467-024-50776-1 resolves to a condensed-matter physics paper on non-Hermitian skin effects (PMID 39095338), not a Wolbachia T4SS study. No mention of WalE1 or any secretion system. Cited DOI does not support the claim.

results.resolved_title: Legionella pneumophila Effector LpdA Is a Palmitoylated Phospholipase D Virulence Factor.
results.quote: The virulence of L. pneumophila depends on its Dot/Icm type IV secretion system (T4SS), which delivers more than 300 effector proteins into the host ... we characterized LpdA, a phospholipase D effector
results.note: Cited DOI 10.1111/j.1462-5822.2007.00928.x (Cell Microbiol 2008) is paywalled (HTTP 402) and could not be retrieved. Quote is from Schroeder et al. 2015 Infect Immun, PMID 26216420, DOI 10.1128/IAI.00785-15, which characterizes LpdA as a Dot/Icm T4SS effector of L. pneumophila.

results.resolved_title: Transferred interbacterial antagonism genes augment eukaryotic innate immune function
results.note: DOI MISMATCH: 10.1038/nature13965 (PMID 25470067) is about Tae/Dae amidase T6SS effectors transferred to eukaryotes; it does not mention Tse6 or locus BTH_I0070. Full text retrieved and searched: no Tse6 evidence. Cited DOI does not support the Tse6 claim.

results.resolved_title: Manganese scavenging and oxidative stress response mediated by type VI secretion system in
results.quote: we identified a T6SS-4–dependent Mn-binding effector TseM, and its interacting partner MnoT, a Mn-specific TonB-dependent outer membrane transporter.
results.note: Cited paper (PMID 28242693) names TseM (BTH_II1883) as a T6SS-secreted Mn-binding effector; matches T6SS claim. Organism (Burkholderia thailandensis) blank in input but is the paper's subject.

results.resolved_title: Sat, the Secreted Autotransporter Toxin of Uropathogenic, Is a Vacuolating Cytotoxin for Bladder and Kidney Epithelial Cells
results.quote: Sat, a vacuolating cytotoxin expressed by uropathogenicCFT073, elicits defined damage to kidney epithelium during upper urinary tract infection
results.note: Cited paper (PMID 12117966). Sat = Secreted Autotransporter Toxin of UPEC CFT073; autotransporters are the T5SS (type V) class, matching ss_type T5SS. Abstract confirms Sat is a secreted toxin.
